And this is what a lot of people don't know, you may already know this, but a lot of people don't know, is that if someone skips the ad between 5 and 29 seconds, you don't pay.

## [27:08 - 30:08]

You only pay if somebody watches that YouTube ad for more than 30 seconds. So what that means is, you know, there's a countdown 5, 4, 3, 2, 1, so after 5, 6 seconds, you can skip the ad. Excuse me, you can skip the ad. But if they skip between that 5 and 29 seconds, you actually don't pay for it. You only pay if they watch more than 30 seconds of that ad. And so just think of the power that that means. If you pull the right person in, push the wrong person away, you don't care if somebody skips the ad because you're not paying for it. Think about how different that is than Facebook. On Facebook, you're paying for every single person that's, you know, seeing you in the news feed. They're scrolling by, right? It's like Tinder, one swipe and you're gone. Versus with YouTube, you actually have 30 seconds to see if somebody is genuinely interested before you pay for the view. So if somebody skips the ad, you don't pay for it. If somebody watches, you pay for it. Now, here's the crazy part. And even people who knew that about the YouTube skip button and love the YouTube skip button, some people don't even know this, right? And this blows. I have a lot of friends who are marketers and media buyers, right? This even blows their minds. The same rules where you only pay if somebody watches 30 seconds of your ad, the same rules apply for your retargeting ads. Think about what that means for a second, right?
